dusthaven-gate.name: Settlement Gate
dusthaven-gate.description:
A wall of scrap metal and old car doors rings the settlement. Two guards watch from behind sandbags, hands resting on makeshift weapons. A hand-painted sign reads: {dim:DUSTHAVEN -- POP. ~200}.

The air smells of woodsmoke and rust. The guards wave you through after a cursory look.

examine.guards: Weathered men with pipe-weapons and sharp eyes. Tired but alert -- the kind who survived by being careful.
examine.wall: Car doors, sheet metal, corrugated iron -- all welded and bolted into a perimeter wall. It wouldn't stop a tank, but it keeps out the wildlife and the desperate.
examine.sign: Hand-painted, carefully lettered. Someone takes pride in this place.

choices.text: Head back to the surface ruins

market-square.name: Market Square
market-square.description:
The heart of Dusthaven. Packed earth surrounded by lean-to stalls where traders hawk salvage, food, and tools. The air is thick with the smell of cooking meat and machine oil. Children chase each other between the stalls.

{purple:Wex} the merchant has a large cart in the center, piled high with goods. He waves when he spots you.

examine.stalls: Electronics, food, clothing, weapons -- the economy of survival, spread across blankets and plywood counters.
examine.children: Three kids playing tag. They look healthy enough. Someone is feeding them, keeping them safe. Life goes on.
examine.square: People trade, gossip, argue, and plan. The center of everything Dusthaven has rebuilt.

workshop.name: Workshop
workshop.description:
An open-air workshop under a corrugated roof. Workbenches overflow with tools, components, and half-finished projects. A {cyan:forge} glows in the back corner, radiating waves of heat.

A {cyan:notice board} near the entrance has several job postings pinned to it.

examine.forge: A makeshift forge built from an old industrial furnace. The tools beside it are well-maintained -- someone knows what they're doing.

board.text:
Job postings on the notice board:

{bright:WANTED:} Scavenger for Spine expedition. Good pay. Dangerous. Ask at tavern.
{bright:WANTED:} Circuit boards, power cells. Will trade food. -- Wex
{pink:WARNING:} Conduit cultists spotted near the antenna. Do NOT approach alone.
{bright:NOTICE:} Anyone with pre-Collapse medical knowledge, report to water tower.

examine.tools: Wrenches, pliers, soldering irons, a hand-cranked drill. The basics of post-apocalyptic engineering.
examine.workbenches: A radio being repaired, a water filter half-assembled, what looks like a weapon modification. Resourceful people.

water-tower.name: Water Tower
water-tower.description:
A converted water tower serves as Dusthaven's medical station and lookout post. A {purple:medic} works at a table covered in salvaged supplies. The sharp smell of antiseptic cuts through the wind up here.

The view stretches to the horizon -- wasteland in every direction. Far to the west, the dark line of an elevated highway cuts across the landscape.

examine.view: Kilometers of visibility. The ruined city behind you, scrubland ahead. The Spine highway is a dark line cutting southwest. Beyond it, something tall catches the light -- the antenna.
examine.medic: A tired-looking woman sorting pill bottles and bandages. She glances at your {purple:neural implant} with professional interest.
examine.supplies: Bandages, antiseptic, a few precious bottles of pre-Collapse medication. Not much for a settlement of 200.

signal.text: You focus on your {purple:implant}. From this height, the signal is clearer: {bright:BEARING 274 WEST -- STRENGTH 4/10}. It's coming from beyond The Spine, past the antenna. Somewhere underground.

tavern.name: The Rusty Valve
tavern.description:
Dusthaven's tavern -- shipping containers welded together into a dim, low-ceilinged space. Mismatched furniture, a bar made from an old service counter, and the sour tang of homebrew in the air.

A {purple:grizzled woman} sits alone in the corner, studying a map spread across her table. She has the look of someone who knows The Spine.

examine.bar: The bartender nods. Homebrew spirits or filtered water. Both cost scrap.

map.text:
The woman's map shows The Spine in detail — safe routes, danger zones, supply caches marked with symbols you don't recognize.

Something about the tactical markings triggers a flash behind your eyes. A {purple:memory fragment} — unbidden, sharp as a knife.

examine.locals: A handful of settlers nursing drinks. They eye you with the casual wariness of people who've learned not to trust strangers quickly.

west-road.name: West Road
west-road.description:
The road out of Dusthaven heading southwest. A weathered sign points the way: {dim:THE SPINE -- 8KM}. A newer sign below it reads: {pink:CAUTION -- BANDIT ACTIVITY REPORTED}.

Wind kicks grit across the packed earth. The road is well-worn -- traders use this route regularly, but it's not without risk.

examine.sign: The Spine is an elevated highway that survived the Collapse mostly intact. The main route west toward Sector 7-G and the antenna.
examine.road: Tire tracks and footprints. Regular traffic. Safety in numbers -- or at least in well-worn paths.
